rawText:
We live in an age of unprecedented access to information, yet the widespread sense that we do not understand more than previous generations did suggests that the relationship between information and understanding is far less straightforward than the architects of the digital revolution assumed.

Information, in its rawest form, is inert: a datum sitting in a database, possessing no capacity to alter the mind of the person who encounters it unless that person brings to it a framework of prior knowledge and evaluative judgment sufficient to transform reception into comprehension.

Understanding, by contrast, is an active cognitive achievement — it requires the learner not merely to receive but to integrate, not merely to store but to connect, and not merely to recall but to apply, a process as much imaginative as it is intellectual.

The confusion between these two states — having access to a fact versus genuinely grasping its significance — has been exacerbated by the metaphors we use for learning: we speak of "acquiring" knowledge, "consuming" content, and "absorbing" information, all of which cast the learner as a passive container rather than an active constructor of meaning.

These metaphors are not innocent; they shape pedagogical practice in ways that systematically privilege accumulation over cultivation, producing students who can retrieve facts on demand but who struggle to synthesize them into coherent frameworks or transfer them to novel contexts.

The philosopher Michael Polanyi drew a useful distinction between "focal" and "subsidiary" awareness: when we truly understand something, individual facts recede into the background while the pattern they compose moves into focal awareness, much as the letters of a word disappear once fluent reading is achieved.

This shift from parts to pattern is not automatic; it requires sustained engagement, deliberate reflection, and a willingness to sit with confusion long enough for the underlying structure to emerge — a willingness that the architecture of modern information systems actively discourages.

Reading offers a particularly instructive case: the reader who skims a complex essay, extracting key claims as bullet points, has acquired information; the reader who rereads the same essay, traces its argumentative structure, and relates its conclusions to her own experience has moved toward understanding.

The difference is not merely quantitative but qualitative: the second reader has engaged in a fundamentally different cognitive activity, one involving not only the intellect but also the capacity for self-reflection that psychologists call metacognition.

Metacognition — thinking about one's own thinking — is arguably the single most important skill separating the informed from the understanding, for it is the mechanism by which a learner monitors comprehension, recognizes gaps, and adjusts strategies accordingly.

Without metacognitive awareness, a reader may finish an article feeling satisfied, only to discover, when asked to explain it, that her understanding was illusory — a phenomenon so common that researchers have named it the "illusion of explanatory depth."

The remedy is not more information but better habits of mind: the discipline to ask "Do I actually understand this, or do I merely recognize it?", the humility to admit confusion, and the patience to pursue understanding even when information alone would suffice.

In language learning, this distinction acquires special urgency, for the learner who memorizes vocabulary and grammar rules has acquired information about the language, while the learner who can think and feel in the target language has achieved understanding of it — and the distance between those two accomplishments is vast.

The aspiration, then, should not be to know more but to understand better, recognizing that understanding is not a destination but a practice — an ongoing, effortful, and ultimately rewarding conversation between the mind and the material it seeks to comprehend.

overview.title: The Difference Between Information and Understanding

sentences.text: Having a lot of information does not mean you understand things better.
sentences.translation: 정보를 많이 가지고 있다고 해서 더 잘 이해하는 것은 아니에요.

sentences.text: Understanding means connecting ideas, not just remembering facts.
sentences.translation: 이해한다는 것은 사실을 기억하는 것이 아니라 아이디어를 연결하는 거예요.

sentences.text: When we say we "absorb" information, we make learning sound too easy and passive.
sentences.translation: 정보를 "흡수한다"고 말할 때, 우리는 학습을 너무 쉽고 수동적인 것처럼 들리게 해요.

sentences.text: You can check if you truly understand something by trying to explain it to someone else.
sentences.translation: 다른 사람에게 설명해 보면 진짜 이해하고 있는지 확인할 수 있어요.

sentences.text: In language learning, knowing words is information, but thinking in the new language is understanding.
sentences.translation: 언어 학습에서 단어를 아는 것은 정보이지만, 새 언어로 생각하는 것은 이해예요.

sentences.text: The goal is not to know more but to understand better.
sentences.translation: 목표는 더 많이 아는 것이 아니라 더 잘 이해하는 거예요.

sentences.text: The essay argues that unprecedented access to information has not produced proportional gains in understanding, revealing a fundamental gap between the two concepts.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 전례 없는 정보 접근이 이해의 비례적 증가를 가져오지 못했으며, 두 개념 사이의 근본적 간극을 드러낸다고 주장해요.

sentences.text: Information is characterized as "inert" — it requires the learner's active frameworks of prior knowledge and judgment to become comprehension.
sentences.translation: 정보는 "비활성적"으로 특성화돼요 — 이해가 되려면 학습자의 사전 지식과 판단의 능동적 틀이 필요해요.

sentences.text: Metaphors like "acquiring" and "absorbing" knowledge are critiqued for casting learners as passive containers rather than active constructors of meaning.
sentences.translation: 지식을 "획득하다"와 "흡수하다" 같은 비유는 학습자를 의미의 능동적 구성자가 아닌 수동적 용기로 설정한다고 비판돼요.

sentences.text: Polanyi's distinction between "focal" and "subsidiary" awareness describes the cognitive shift from individual facts to integrated patterns that characterizes genuine understanding.
sentences.translation: 폴라니의 "초점적"과 "보조적" 인식의 구분은 진정한 이해를 특징짓는 개별 사실에서 통합된 패턴으로의 인지적 전환을 묘사해요.

sentences.text: Metacognition — thinking about one's own thinking — is identified as the critical skill separating surface-level information retention from deep understanding.
sentences.translation: 메타인지 — 자신의 사고에 대해 생각하는 것 — 는 표면적 정보 보유와 깊은 이해를 구분하는 핵심 기술로 확인돼요.

sentences.text: The essay concludes by applying the information-understanding distinction to language learning, arguing that thinking in a language represents understanding while memorizing rules represents mere information.
sentences.translation: 에세이는 정보-이해 구분을 언어 학습에 적용하며, 언어로 생각하는 것이 이해를 대표하는 반면 규칙을 암기하는 것은 단순한 정보를 대표한다고 결론지어요.

sentences.text: This essay operates at the intersection of epistemology, cognitive psychology, and pedagogy, arguing that the information-understanding distinction is not merely conceptual but has material consequences for how institutions design learning environments.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 인식론, 인지 심리학, 교육학의 교차점에서 작동하며, 정보-이해 구분이 단순히 개념적인 것이 아니라 기관이 학습 환경을 설계하는 방식에 실질적 결과를 가진다고 논증해요.

sentences.text: The critique of learning metaphors exemplifies the Sapir-Whorf insight that language shapes thought: the metaphors we use for learning constrain what we believe learning can be.
sentences.translation: 학습 비유에 대한 비판은 언어가 사고를 형성한다는 사피어-워프 통찰을 예시해요: 학습에 사용하는 비유가 학습이 무엇일 수 있는지에 대한 우리의 믿음을 제약해요.

sentences.text: Polanyi's focal/subsidiary distinction provides the essay's epistemological backbone, articulating the qualitative difference between data accumulation and pattern recognition that lies at the heart of understanding.
sentences.translation: 폴라니의 초점적/보조적 구분은 에세이의 인식론적 척추를 제공하며, 이해의 핵심에 놓인 데이터 축적과 패턴 인식 사이의 질적 차이를 분절해요.

sentences.text: The "illusion of explanatory depth" functions as empirical evidence for the essay's philosophical claim: recognition is cognitively distinct from comprehension, and without metacognitive monitoring, the learner cannot distinguish between them.
sentences.translation: "설명적 깊이의 착각"은 에세이의 철학적 주장에 대한 경험적 증거로 기능해요: 인식은 이해와 인지적으로 구별되며, 메타인지적 모니터링 없이는 학습자가 둘을 구분할 수 없어요.

sentences.text: The application to language learning performs a reflexive function: readers of this English-language essay are themselves engaged in the very transition from information to understanding that the text describes.
sentences.translation: 언어 학습에의 적용은 반성적 기능을 수행해요: 이 영어 에세이의 독자들은 그 텍스트가 묘사하는 정보에서 이해로의 바로 그 전환에 참여하고 있어요.

sentences.text: The closing redefinition of understanding as "practice" rather than "destination" aligns with process philosophy and growth mindset theory, positioning comprehension as an ongoing activity rather than a terminal state.
sentences.translation: 이해를 "목적지"가 아닌 "실천"으로 재정의하는 마무리는 과정 철학 및 성장 마인드셋 이론과 일치하며, 이해를 최종 상태가 아닌 지속적 활동으로 위치시켜요.

sentences.original: We live in an age of unprecedented access to information, yet the widespread sense that we do not understand more than previous generations did suggests that the relationship between information and understanding is far less straightforward than the architects of the digital revolution assumed.
sentences.translation: 우리는 전례 없는 정보 접근의 시대에 살고 있지만, 우리가 이전 세대보다 더 많이 이해하지 못한다는 광범위한 감각은 정보와 이해의 관계가 디지털 혁명의 설계자들이 가정했던 것보다 훨씬 덜 직선적임을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: We live in an age of unprecedented access to information
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: yet the widespread sense
grammarElements.label: 대조절 주어

grammarElements.text: that we do not understand more than previous generations did
grammarElements.label: 동격 that절

grammarElements.text: suggests that the relationship between information and understanding is far less straightforward
grammarElements.label: 동사 + that 명사절

grammarElements.text: than the architects of the digital revolution assumed
grammarElements.label: 비교절

vocabulary.example: We have unprecedented access to information.
The relationship is far less straightforward than assumed.

expressions.usage: 사회적 인식을 학술적으로 도입하는 표현

expressions.phrase: far less straightforward than X assumed
expressions.meaning: X가 가정했던 것보다 훨씬 덜 직선적인
expressions.usage: 기대와 현실의 괴리를 학술적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: We live in an age of [unprecedented X], yet [the widespread sense that Y] suggests that [the relationship between A and B] is far less [straightforward] than [Z assumed]
patterns.explanation: 시대적 배경을 설정하고, "yet"으로 역설을 도입한 뒤, "far less straightforward than"으로 핵심 문제를 선언하는 도입 패턴.
examples: We live in an age of unprecedented connectivity, yet the widespread sense that we are lonelier than ever suggests that the relationship between connection and belonging is far less straightforward than the designers of social platforms assumed.

We live in an age of unprecedented educational access, yet the widespread sense that critical thinking has not improved suggests that the relationship between access and learning is far less straightforward than policymakers assumed.

drillQuestions.question: "Unprecedented" means never _____ before.
"Yet" introduces a _____ to the first clause.
"Far less straightforward" means much more _____.
"The architects of the digital revolution" refers to _____.

sentences.notes: 시대적 역설 — 정보의 증가가 이해의 증가로 이어지지 않는다 — 로 에세이를 시작한다. "the widespread sense"가 경험적 관찰을, "far less straightforward than"이 이론적 문제 제기를 수행한다.

koreanAlignment.en: We live in an age of unprecedented access to information
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리는 전례 없는 정보 접근의 시대에 살고 있지만

koreanAlignment.en: yet the widespread sense that we do not understand more than previous generations did
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리가 이전 세대보다 더 많이 이해하지 못한다는 광범위한 감각은

koreanAlignment.en: suggests that the relationship between information and understanding
koreanAlignment.ko: 정보와 이해의 관계가

koreanAlignment.en: is far less straightforward than the architects of the digital revolution assumed
koreanAlignment.ko: 디지털 혁명의 설계자들이 가정했던 것보다 훨씬 덜 직선적임을 시사한다

pronunciationNotes: unprecedented: /ʌnˈpresɪdentɪd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "언프레시덴티드"에 가깝다.
straightforward: /ˌstreɪtˈfɔːrwərd/ — 두 번째 요소에 강세. "스트레이트포워드"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Information, in its rawest form, is inert: a datum sitting in a database, possessing no capacity to alter the mind of the person who encounters it unless that person brings to it a framework of prior knowledge and evaluative judgment sufficient to transform reception into comprehension.
sentences.translation: 정보는, 가장 원시적 형태에서, 비활성적이다: 데이터베이스에 놓인 데이터, 그것을 접하는 사람이 수용을 이해로 변환하기에 충분한 사전 지식과 평가적 판단의 틀을 가져오지 않는 한, 그 사람의 마음을 변화시킬 능력을 보유하지 않는다.

grammarElements.text: Information, in its rawest form,
grammarElements.label: 주어 (삽입 부사구)

grammarElements.text: is inert
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: a datum sitting in a database
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 동격구 (분사구문)

grammarElements.text: possessing no capacity to alter the mind of the person who encounters it
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (관계절 포함)

grammarElements.text: unless that person brings to it a framework of prior knowledge and evaluative judgment sufficient to transform reception into comprehension
grammarElements.label: unless 조건절

vocabulary.example: Information without interpretation is inert.
Evaluative judgment transforms raw data into understanding.
Reception must be transformed into comprehension.

expressions.usage: 대상의 가장 기본적 상태를 지시하는 삽입 부사구
수동적 수용에서 능동적 이해로의 전환을 표현하는 학술적 표현

patterns.pattern: [X], in its rawest form, is [inert]: [a datum/object], possessing no capacity to [change Y] unless [Y brings a framework sufficient to transform A into B]
patterns.explanation: 대상의 본질적 비활성을 선언하고, unless 조건절로 활성화의 조건을 밝히는 정의적 패턴.
examples: A text, in its rawest form, is inert: marks on a page, possessing no capacity to communicate unless the reader brings to them a framework of linguistic knowledge and cultural context sufficient to transform symbols into meaning.

Data, in its rawest form, is inert: numbers in a spreadsheet, possessing no capacity to guide decisions unless the analyst brings to them a framework of domain expertise and interpretive skill sufficient to transform figures into insight.

drillQuestions.question: "Inert" means lacking the power to _____.
"Evaluative judgment" means the ability to _____ quality.
"Unless" introduces a _____ for activation.
"Transform reception into comprehension" describes changing passive _____ into active understanding.

sentences.notes: "inert"가 정보의 본질적 수동성을 강력한 과학적 비유로 선언한다. unless 조건절이 학습자의 능동적 역할을 강조하며, "transform reception into comprehension"이 수동에서 능동으로의 전환을 정확히 명명한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Information, in its rawest form, is inert
koreanAlignment.ko: 정보는, 가장 원시적 형태에서, 비활성적이다

koreanAlignment.en: a datum sitting in a database
koreanAlignment.ko: 데이터베이스에 놓인 데이터

koreanAlignment.en: possessing no capacity to alter the mind of the person who encounters it
koreanAlignment.ko: 그것을 접하는 사람의 마음을 변화시킬 능력을 보유하지 않는다

koreanAlignment.en: unless that person brings to it a framework of prior knowledge and evaluative judgment
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 사람이 사전 지식과 평가적 판단의 틀을 가져오지 않는 한

koreanAlignment.en: sufficient to transform reception into comprehension
koreanAlignment.ko: 수용을 이해로 변환하기에 충분한

pronunciationNotes: inert: /ɪˈnɜːrt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이너트"에 가깝다.
evaluative: /ɪˈvæljuətɪv/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이밸류어티브"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Understanding, by contrast, is an active cognitive achievement — it requires the learner not merely to receive but to integrate, not merely to store but to connect, and not merely to recall but to apply, a process as much imaginative as it is intellectual.
sentences.translation: 이해는, 대조적으로, 능동적 인지적 성취이다 — 학습자에게 단순히 수용이 아닌 통합을, 단순히 저장이 아닌 연결을, 단순히 회상이 아닌 적용을 요구하며, 지적인 것만큼이나 상상적인 과정이다.

grammarElements.text: Understanding, by contrast,
grammarElements.label: 주어 (삽입 접속부사)

grammarElements.text: is an active cognitive achievement
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: it requires the learner not merely to receive but to integrate, not merely to store but to connect, and not merely to recall but to apply
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 설명절 (삼중 "not merely A but B")

grammarElements.text: a process as much imaginative as it is intellectual
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구 (as much A as B)

vocabulary.example: Understanding is a cognitive achievement, not a passive state.
Understanding requires integrating new information with existing knowledge.

expressions.usage: 앞 문장과의 대비를 명시하는 삽입 접속부사

expressions.phrase: not merely to A but to B
expressions.meaning: 단순히 A하는 것이 아니라 B하는 것
expressions.usage: "not merely ... but" 구조를 삼중 반복하여 능동적 행위의 요구를 강조

patterns.pattern: [Understanding], by contrast, is an active [achievement] — it requires not merely to [A] but to [B], not merely to [C] but to [D], and not merely to [E] but to [F]
patterns.explanation: "by contrast"로 앞 문장(정보)과 대비하고, 삼중 "not merely ... but" 구조로 이해의 능동적 요구를 체계적으로 나열하는 패턴.
examples: Expertise, by contrast, is an active cognitive construction — it requires the practitioner not merely to follow rules but to adapt, not merely to recognize patterns but to create, and not merely to perform tasks but to innovate.

Wisdom, by contrast, is an active moral achievement — it requires the individual not merely to know facts but to weigh them, not merely to identify options but to judge them, and not merely to decide but to accept consequences.

drillQuestions.question: "By contrast" signals a _____ with the previous sentence.
"Integrate" means to _____ parts into a whole.
The pattern uses _____ "not merely ... but" pairs.
"As much imaginative as intellectual" means understanding requires _____.

sentences.notes: 삼중 "not merely ... but" 구조(수용→통합, 저장→연결, 회상→적용)가 이해의 능동적 요구를 체계적으로 나열한다. "as much imaginative as intellectual"이 이해의 감성적·상상적 차원을 추가하여 순수 인지주의를 넘어선다.

koreanAlignment.en: Understanding, by contrast, is an active cognitive achievement
koreanAlignment.ko: 이해는 대조적으로 능동적 인지적 성취이다

koreanAlignment.en: it requires the learner not merely to receive but to integrate
koreanAlignment.ko: 학습자에게 단순히 수용이 아닌 통합을 요구하며

koreanAlignment.en: not merely to store but to connect, and not merely to recall but to apply
koreanAlignment.ko: 단순히 저장이 아닌 연결을, 단순히 회상이 아닌 적용을

koreanAlignment.en: a process as much imaginative as it is intellectual
koreanAlignment.ko: 지적인 것만큼이나 상상적인 과정이다

pronunciationNotes: cognitive: /ˈkɑːɡnɪtɪv/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "카그니티브"에 가깝다.
integrate: /ˈɪntɪɡreɪt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "인티그레이트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The confusion between these two states — having access to a fact versus genuinely grasping its significance — has been exacerbated by the metaphors we use for learning: we speak of "acquiring" knowledge, "consuming" content, and "absorbing" information, all of which cast the learner as a passive container rather than an active constructor of meaning.
sentences.translation: 이 두 상태 사이의 혼동 — 사실에 접근하는 것 대 그 의미를 진정으로 파악하는 것 — 은 우리가 학습에 사용하는 비유에 의해 악화되어 왔다: 우리는 지식을 "획득하고", 콘텐츠를 "소비하고", 정보를 "흡수한다"고 말하며, 이 모든 것이 학습자를 의미의 능동적 구성자가 아닌 수동적 용기로 설정한다.

grammarElements.text: The confusion between these two states
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: having access to a fact versus genuinely grasping its significance
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격구

grammarElements.text: has been exacerbated by the metaphors we use for learning
grammarElements.label: 동사 (현재완료 수동태)

grammarElements.text: we speak of "acquiring" knowledge, "consuming" content, and "absorbing" information
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 예시절 (삼중 인용)

grammarElements.text: all of which cast the learner as a passive container rather than an active constructor of meaning
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절

vocabulary.example: Our learning metaphors have exacerbated the confusion.
Passive metaphors cast the learner as a container to be filled.

expressions.usage: 문제의 원인을 지적하는 현재완료 수동태 표현
비유가 학습자의 역할을 어떻게 규정하는지 비판하는 표현

patterns.pattern: The confusion between [A and B] has been exacerbated by the metaphors we use: we speak of "[X]," "[Y]," and "[Z]," all of which cast [the person] as [passive role] rather than [active role]
patterns.explanation: 혼동의 원인을 비유 분석으로 진단하고, 삼중 인용 예시와 관계절로 비유의 문제적 함의를 밝히는 패턴.
examples: The confusion between correlation and causation has been exacerbated by the metaphors journalists use: they speak of factors "driving" trends, studies "proving" links, and data "showing" causes, all of which cast statistical association as deterministic mechanism rather than probabilistic relationship.

The confusion between disagreement and hostility has been exacerbated by the metaphors we use for debate: we speak of "attacking" positions, "defending" claims, and "winning" arguments, all of which cast intellectual exchange as combat rather than collaborative inquiry.

drillQuestions.question: "Exacerbated" means made _____.
The three metaphors — acquiring, consuming, absorbing — are all _____.
"Active constructor of meaning" is contrasted with passive _____.
The argument is that metaphors _____ how we think about learning.

sentences.notes: 학습 비유에 대한 메타언어적 분석이 에세이의 독창적 기여이다. "acquiring/consuming/absorbing"의 삼중 인용이 수동성의 패턴을 노출하고, "passive container vs. active constructor"가 대안적 프레임을 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The confusion between these two states
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 두 상태 사이의 혼동은

koreanAlignment.en: having access to a fact versus genuinely grasping its significance
koreanAlignment.ko: 사실에 접근하는 것 대 그 의미를 진정으로 파악하는 것

koreanAlignment.en: has been exacerbated by the metaphors we use for learning
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리가 학습에 사용하는 비유에 의해 악화되어 왔다

koreanAlignment.en: we speak of "acquiring" knowledge, "consuming" content, and "absorbing" information
koreanAlignment.ko: 지식을 "획득하고", 콘텐츠를 "소비하고", 정보를 "흡수한다"고 말하며

koreanAlignment.en: all of which cast the learner as a passive container rather than an active constructor of meaning
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 모든 것이 학습자를 의미의 능동적 구성자가 아닌 수동적 용기로 설정한다

pronunciationNotes: exacerbated: /ɪɡˈzæsərbeɪtɪd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이그재서베이티드"에 가깝다.
container: /kənˈteɪnər/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨테이너"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: These metaphors are not innocent; they shape pedagogical practice in ways that systematically privilege accumulation over cultivation, producing students who can retrieve facts on demand but who struggle to synthesize them into coherent frameworks or transfer them to novel contexts.
sentences.translation: 이 비유들은 무해하지 않다; 그것들은 축적을 배양보다 체계적으로 우선시하는 방식으로 교육적 실천을 형성하여, 요구에 따라 사실을 인출할 수 있지만 그것들을 일관된 틀로 종합하거나 새로운 맥락으로 전이하는 데 어려움을 겪는 학생들을 생산한다.

grammarElements.text: These metaphors are not innocent
grammarElements.label: 주절 (리토테스)

grammarElements.text: they shape pedagogical practice in ways that systematically privilege accumulation over cultivation
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 설명절 (관계절)

grammarElements.text: producing students who can retrieve facts on demand but who struggle to synthesize them into coherent frameworks or transfer them to novel contexts
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (관계절 + 병렬)

vocabulary.example: Learning metaphors shape pedagogical practice.
Students struggle to synthesize facts into frameworks.
Education should prioritize cultivation over accumulation.

expressions.usage: 리토테스로 비유의 실질적 해악을 강조하는 표현
두 교육 방향의 대비를 표현하는 학술적 표현

patterns.pattern: These [metaphors] are not innocent; they shape [practice] in ways that systematically privilege [X over Y], producing [people] who can [A] but who struggle to [B or C]
patterns.explanation: 리토테스로 비유의 해악을 선언하고, "privilege X over Y"로 체계적 편향을 밝힌 뒤, "can A but struggle to B"로 결과를 구체화하는 패턴.
examples: These labels are not innocent; they shape hiring practice in ways that systematically privilege credentials over capability, producing professionals who can display qualifications but who struggle to solve problems creatively or adapt to unexpected challenges.

These norms are not innocent; they shape organizational culture in ways that systematically privilege compliance over innovation, producing teams that can follow procedures but who struggle to question assumptions or propose alternatives.

drillQuestions.question: "Are not innocent" is a _____ meaning they cause harm.
"Pedagogical" relates to _____.
"Privilege accumulation over cultivation" means favoring _____ over growing.
Students can "retrieve" facts but struggle to _____ them.

sentences.notes: "are not innocent"의 리토테스가 비유의 실질적 해악을 경고한다. "accumulation vs. cultivation"의 대비가 에세이의 핵심 이분법을 교육적 맥락에서 구체화하고, "retrieve ... but struggle to synthesize"가 그 결과를 학생의 역량 프로필로 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: These metaphors are not innocent
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 비유들은 무해하지 않다

koreanAlignment.en: they shape pedagogical practice in ways that systematically privilege accumulation over cultivation
koreanAlignment.ko: 축적을 배양보다 체계적으로 우선시하는 방식으로 교육적 실천을 형성하여

koreanAlignment.en: producing students who can retrieve facts on demand
koreanAlignment.ko: 요구에 따라 사실을 인출할 수 있지만

koreanAlignment.en: but who struggle to synthesize them into coherent frameworks or transfer them to novel contexts
koreanAlignment.ko: 그것들을 일관된 틀로 종합하거나 새로운 맥락으로 전이하는 데 어려움을 겪는 학생들을 생산한다

pronunciationNotes: pedagogical: /ˌpedəˈɡɑːdʒɪkəl/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "페더가지컬"에 가깝다.
synthesize: /ˈsɪnθəsaɪz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "신서사이즈"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The philosopher Michael Polanyi drew a useful distinction between "focal" and "subsidiary" awareness: when we truly understand something, individual facts recede into the background while the pattern they compose moves into focal awareness, much as the letters of a word disappear once fluent reading is achieved.
sentences.translation: 철학자 마이클 폴라니는 "초점적"과 "보조적" 인식 사이에 유용한 구분을 그었다: 우리가 무언가를 진정으로 이해할 때, 개별 사실은 배경으로 물러나고 그것들이 구성하는 패턴이 초점 인식으로 이동한다 — 유창한 독서가 달성되면 단어의 글자가 사라지는 것과 같이.

grammarElements.text: The philosopher Michael Polanyi drew a useful distinction between "focal" and "subsidiary" awareness
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: when we truly understand something, individual facts recede into the background
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 시간절 + 주절 1

grammarElements.text: while the pattern they compose moves into focal awareness
grammarElements.label: while 대조절

grammarElements.text: much as the letters of a word disappear once fluent reading is achieved
grammarElements.label: much as 비교절

vocabulary.example: Understanding shifts attention to focal awareness of patterns.
Individual facts become subsidiary when understanding emerges.
Facts recede into the background when the pattern emerges.

expressions.usage: 학술적 개념 도입 시 쓰는 격식적 동사구
유비적 비교를 도입하는 접속사적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Philosopher] drew a useful distinction between "[A]" and "[B]": when [we understand], [individual parts recede] while [the pattern moves into focus], much as [concrete analogy]
patterns.explanation: 철학적 구분을 소개하고, 콜론 뒤에서 설명한 뒤, "much as"로 직관적 유비를 제공하는 학술적 개념 도입 패턴.
examples: The psychologist Daniel Kahneman drew a useful distinction between "System 1" and "System 2" thinking: when we encounter familiar problems, effortful analysis recedes while automatic recognition takes over, much as a seasoned driver navigates traffic without conscious deliberation.

The linguist Noam Chomsky drew a useful distinction between "competence" and "performance": when we speak fluently, explicit grammar rules recede into the background while communicative intent moves into focal awareness, much as musical scales disappear once a pianist performs a piece.

drillQuestions.question: "Focal" awareness is awareness in the _____.
"Subsidiary" means playing a _____ role.
"Recede" means to move _____.
The letter-word analogy illustrates the shift from _____ to pattern.

sentences.notes: 폴라니의 초점적/보조적 인식 구분이 이해의 인지적 메커니즘을 밝힌다. 글자-단어 유비가 추상적 철학 개념을 즉각적으로 접근 가능하게 만들며 — 기사 자체가 과학 대중화와 같은 "재서사화" 전략을 수행하고 있다.

koreanAlignment.en: The philosopher Michael Polanyi drew a useful distinction between "focal" and "subsidiary" awareness
koreanAlignment.ko: 철학자 마이클 폴라니는 "초점적"과 "보조적" 인식 사이에 유용한 구분을 그었다

koreanAlignment.en: when we truly understand something, individual facts recede into the background
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리가 무언가를 진정으로 이해할 때, 개별 사실은 배경으로 물러나고

koreanAlignment.en: while the pattern they compose moves into focal awareness
koreanAlignment.ko: 그것들이 구성하는 패턴이 초점 인식으로 이동한다

koreanAlignment.en: much as the letters of a word disappear once fluent reading is achieved
koreanAlignment.ko: 유창한 독서가 달성되면 단어의 글자가 사라지는 것과 같이

pronunciationNotes: Polanyi: /poʊˈlɑːnji/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "폴라니"에 가깝다.
subsidiary: /səbˈsɪdiˌeri/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "서브시디어리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: This shift from parts to pattern is not automatic; it requires sustained engagement, deliberate reflection, and a willingness to sit with confusion long enough for the underlying structure to emerge — a willingness that the architecture of modern information systems actively discourages.
sentences.translation: 부분에서 패턴으로의 이 전환은 자동적이지 않다; 그것은 지속적 참여, 의도적 성찰, 그리고 기저의 구조가 출현하기에 충분히 오래 혼란과 함께 앉아 있으려는 의지를 요구한다 — 현대 정보 시스템의 구조가 적극적으로 저해하는 의지.

grammarElements.text: This shift from parts to pattern
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is not automatic
grammarElements.label: 보어 (부정)

grammarElements.text: it requires sustained engagement, deliberate reflection, and a willingness to sit with confusion long enough for the underlying structure to emerge
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 설명절 (삼중 병렬)

grammarElements.text: a willingness that the architecture of modern information systems actively discourages
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 동격 관계절

vocabulary.example: Understanding requires sustained engagement, not brief exposure.
Modern information systems actively discourage deep engagement.

expressions.usage: 불확실성을 견디는 인지적 태도를 비유적으로 표현

expressions.phrase: the architecture of modern information systems
expressions.usage: 디지털 환경의 구조적 특성을 기술하는 학술적 표현

patterns.pattern: This [shift] is not automatic; it requires [A], [B], and [a willingness to C] — a willingness that [modern systems] actively discourages
patterns.explanation: 부정("not automatic")으로 오해를 차단하고, 삼중 병렬로 요구 조건을 나열한 뒤, 대시 뒤 관계절로 현대적 장애물을 지적하는 패턴.
examples: This maturity is not automatic; it requires honest self-assessment, consistent practice, and a willingness to accept criticism long enough for genuine improvement to emerge — a willingness that the culture of instant validation actively discourages.

This trust is not automatic; it requires transparency, accountability, and a willingness to be vulnerable long enough for authentic connection to develop — a willingness that the pace of modern professional life actively discourages.

drillQuestions.question: "Sit with confusion" means to _____ uncertainty.
"Sustained engagement" means _____ involvement.
Modern information systems "actively discourage" means they _____ against patience.
The dash introduces what makes the required willingness _____.

sentences.notes: "sit with confusion"이라는 생생한 비유가 이해의 인내적 측면을 포착한다. 대시 뒤의 관계절이 현대 정보 시스템의 구조적 방해를 지적하여 개인의 의지 문제를 시스템 문제로 확장한다.

koreanAlignment.en: This shift from parts to pattern is not automatic
koreanAlignment.ko: 부분에서 패턴으로의 이 전환은 자동적이지 않다

koreanAlignment.en: it requires sustained engagement, deliberate reflection
koreanAlignment.ko: 지속적 참여, 의도적 성찰을 요구한다

koreanAlignment.en: and a willingness to sit with confusion long enough for the underlying structure to emerge
koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 기저의 구조가 출현하기에 충분히 오래 혼란과 함께 앉아 있으려는 의지를

koreanAlignment.en: a willingness that the architecture of modern information systems actively discourages
koreanAlignment.ko: 현대 정보 시스템의 구조가 적극적으로 저해하는 의지

pronunciationNotes: sustained: /səˈsteɪnd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "서스테인드"에 가깝다.
discourages: /dɪsˈkɜːrɪdʒɪz/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "디스커리지즈"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Reading offers a particularly instructive case: the reader who skims a complex essay, extracting key claims as bullet points, has acquired information; the reader who rereads the same essay, traces its argumentative structure, and relates its conclusions to her own experience has moved toward understanding.
sentences.translation: 독서는 특히 교훈적인 사례를 제공한다: 복잡한 에세이를 훑어보며 핵심 주장을 글머리 기호로 추출하는 독자는 정보를 획득한 것이고; 같은 에세이를 재독하며 논증 구조를 추적하고 그 결론을 자신의 경험과 연결하는 독자는 이해를 향해 이동한 것이다.

grammarElements.text: Reading offers a particularly instructive case
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: the reader who skims a complex essay, extracting key claims as bullet points, has acquired information
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 사례 1 (관계절 + 분사구문)

grammarElements.text: the reader who rereads the same essay, traces its argumentative structure, and relates its conclusions to her own experience has moved toward understanding
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 사례 2 (관계절 + 삼중 동사)

vocabulary.example: Skimming an essay gives information, not understanding.
Tracing the argumentative structure leads to deeper understanding.

expressions.usage: 논점을 구체화하는 사례를 도입하는 학술적 표현
이해를 과정으로 표현하는 동적 표현. 도달이 아닌 이동을 강조.

patterns.pattern: [Activity] offers a particularly instructive case: [person who does A] has acquired [information]; [person who does B, C, and D] has moved toward [understanding]
patterns.explanation: 구체적 사례를 통해 정보 획득과 이해 추구의 차이를 대비적으로 보여주는 패턴. 세미콜론이 두 독자를 구조적으로 대비시킨다.
examples: Travel offers a particularly instructive case: the tourist who photographs landmarks and collects souvenirs has acquired memories; the traveler who learns local customs, engages with residents, and reflects on cultural differences has moved toward understanding.

Cooking offers a particularly instructive case: the person who follows a recipe step by step has acquired a skill; the cook who understands why each ingredient matters, adjusts proportions intuitively, and creates new dishes has moved toward understanding.

drillQuestions.question: "Skims" means to read _____.
Extracting bullet points produces _____, not understanding.
"Moved toward understanding" implies understanding is a _____.
The two readers are contrasted using a _____.

sentences.notes: 독서의 두 양식이 정보-이해 구분의 구체적 사례로 제시된다. "has acquired information / has moved toward understanding"의 대비가 동일 텍스트에 대한 두 독자의 질적으로 다른 관계를 보여준다. "moved toward"가 이해를 과정으로 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Reading offers a particularly instructive case
koreanAlignment.ko: 독서는 특히 교훈적인 사례를 제공한다

koreanAlignment.en: the reader who skims a complex essay, extracting key claims as bullet points, has acquired information
koreanAlignment.ko: 복잡한 에세이를 훑어보며 핵심 주장을 글머리 기호로 추출하는 독자는 정보를 획득한 것이고

koreanAlignment.en: the reader who rereads the same essay, traces its argumentative structure, and relates its conclusions to her own experience
koreanAlignment.ko: 같은 에세이를 재독하며 논증 구조를 추적하고 그 결론을 자신의 경험과 연결하는 독자는

koreanAlignment.en: has moved toward understanding
koreanAlignment.ko: 이해를 향해 이동한 것이다

pronunciationNotes: argumentative: /ˌɑːrɡjəˈmentətɪv/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "아규멘터티브"에 가깝다.
instructive: /ɪnˈstrʌktɪv/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인스트럭티브"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The difference is not merely quantitative but qualitative: the second reader has engaged in a fundamentally different cognitive activity, one involving not only the intellect but also the capacity for self-reflection that psychologists call metacognition.
sentences.translation: 차이는 단순히 양적인 것이 아니라 질적인 것이다: 두 번째 독자는 근본적으로 다른 인지 활동에 참여한 것이며, 지성뿐만 아니라 심리학자들이 메타인지라고 부르는 자기 성찰의 능력도 포함하는 활동이다.

grammarElements.text: The difference is not merely quantitative but qualitative
grammarElements.label: 주절 (not merely A but B)

grammarElements.text: the second reader has engaged in a fundamentally different cognitive activity
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절

grammarElements.text: one involving not only the intellect but also the capacity for self-reflection that psychologists call metacognition
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구 (not only A but also B + 관계절)

vocabulary.example: Metacognition — thinking about thinking — is essential for deep understanding.
The difference between information and understanding is qualitative, not quantitative.

expressions.usage: 차이의 본질이 양이 아닌 질에 있음을 강조하는 학술적 대비
전문 용어를 도입할 때 쓰는 관계절 표현

patterns.pattern: The difference is not merely [quantitative] but [qualitative]: [the second X] has engaged in a fundamentally different [activity], one involving not only [A] but also [B that experts call C]
patterns.explanation: 차이의 질적 본질을 선언하고, 콜론 뒤에서 구체화하며, "not only ... but also" + 관계절로 전문 용어를 도입하는 패턴.
examples: The difference is not merely quantitative but qualitative: the second approach has produced a fundamentally different result, one involving not only technical precision but also the creative intuition that practitioners call "design thinking."

The difference is not merely one of degree but of kind: the expert has developed a fundamentally different relationship with the material, one involving not only factual knowledge but also the intuitive pattern recognition that researchers call "chunking."

drillQuestions.question: "Qualitative" difference means a difference in _____.
"Metacognition" means thinking about one's own _____.
The difference involves not only intellect but also _____.
"Fundamentally different" means different at the _____ level.

sentences.notes: "not merely quantitative but qualitative"가 정보-이해 차이를 양적 문제에서 질적 문제로 격상시킨다. "metacognition"이라는 핵심 개념이 관계절을 통해 자연스럽게 도입되며, 다음 문장의 상세 설명을 예고한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The difference is not merely quantitative but qualitative
koreanAlignment.ko: 차이는 단순히 양적인 것이 아니라 질적인 것이다

koreanAlignment.en: the second reader has engaged in a fundamentally different cognitive activity
koreanAlignment.ko: 두 번째 독자는 근본적으로 다른 인지 활동에 참여한 것이며

koreanAlignment.en: one involving not only the intellect but also the capacity for self-reflection
koreanAlignment.ko: 지성뿐만 아니라 자기 성찰의 능력도 포함하는

koreanAlignment.en: that psychologists call metacognition
koreanAlignment.ko: 심리학자들이 메타인지라고 부르는

pronunciationNotes: metacognition: /ˌmetəkɑːɡˈnɪʃən/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "메타카그니션"에 가깝다.
qualitative: /ˈkwɑːlɪteɪtɪv/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "콸리테이티브"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Metacognition — thinking about one's own thinking — is arguably the single most important skill separating the informed from the understanding, for it is the mechanism by which a learner monitors comprehension, recognizes gaps, and adjusts strategies accordingly.
sentences.translation: 메타인지 — 자신의 사고에 대해 생각하는 것 — 는 정보를 가진 자와 이해하는 자를 분리하는 가장 중요한 단일 기술이라고 할 수 있는데, 학습자가 이해를 모니터링하고, 간극을 인식하고, 그에 따라 전략을 조정하는 메커니즘이기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: Metacognition
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: thinking about one's own thinking
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격구 (정의)

grammarElements.text: is arguably the single most important skill separating the informed from the understanding
grammarElements.label: 보어 (최상급 + 분사 수식)

grammarElements.text: for it is the mechanism by which a learner monitors comprehension, recognizes gaps, and adjusts strategies accordingly
grammarElements.label: for 이유절 (삼중 동사)

vocabulary.example: Metacognition is the mechanism that monitors comprehension.
Metacognitive awareness helps learners recognize gaps in understanding.

expressions.usage: 메타인지의 간결한 일상적 정의
형용사의 명사적 용법으로 두 집단을 대비하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Concept] — [simple definition] — is arguably the single most important skill separating [A from B], for it is the mechanism by which [a learner does X, Y, and Z]
patterns.explanation: 대시 삽입 정의로 전문 용어를 접근 가능하게 만들고, "the single most important"로 중요성을 강조한 뒤, for절에서 메커니즘을 설명하는 패턴.
examples: Empathy — the ability to feel what another person feels — is arguably the single most important quality separating effective leaders from merely competent ones, for it is the mechanism by which a leader anticipates needs, builds trust, and inspires loyalty.

Curiosity — the impulse to understand what one does not yet know — is arguably the single most important trait separating lifelong learners from passive recipients, for it is the mechanism by which a person seeks challenges, asks questions, and pursues depth.

drillQuestions.question: "Metacognition" literally means _____ about cognition.
"The informed" and "the understanding" represent two _____ of learners.
"Monitors, recognizes, adjusts" describes a _____ cycle.
"Arguably" indicates the claim is _____ but defensible.

sentences.notes: 대시 삽입 정의가 "metacognition"을 즉시 접근 가능하게 만든다. "the informed from the understanding"의 대비가 에세이의 핵심 이분법을 인격화하고, "monitors, recognizes, adjusts"의 삼중 동사가 메타인지의 순환적 메커니즘을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Metacognition — thinking about one's own thinking —
koreanAlignment.ko: 메타인지 — 자신의 사고에 대해 생각하는 것 — 는

koreanAlignment.en: is arguably the single most important skill separating the informed from the understanding
koreanAlignment.ko: 정보를 가진 자와 이해하는 자를 분리하는 가장 중요한 단일 기술이라고 할 수 있는데

koreanAlignment.en: for it is the mechanism by which a learner monitors comprehension
koreanAlignment.ko: 학습자가 이해를 모니터링하고

koreanAlignment.en: recognizes gaps, and adjusts strategies accordingly
koreanAlignment.ko: 간극을 인식하고, 그에 따라 전략을 조정하는 메커니즘이기 때문이다

pronunciationNotes: monitors: /ˈmɑːnɪtərz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "마니터즈"에 가깝다.
accordingly: /əˈkɔːrdɪŋli/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어코딩리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Without metacognitive awareness, a reader may finish an article feeling satisfied, only to discover, when asked to explain it, that her understanding was illusory — a phenomenon so common that researchers have named it the "illusion of explanatory depth."
sentences.translation: 메타인지적 인식 없이는, 독자가 만족감을 느끼며 기사를 마칠 수 있지만, 그것을 설명하라는 요청을 받았을 때 자신의 이해가 환상적이었음을 발견하게 된다 — 연구자들이 "설명적 깊이의 착각"이라고 이름 붙인 만큼 흔한 현상.

grammarElements.text: Without metacognitive awareness
grammarElements.label: 조건적 전치사구

grammarElements.text: a reader may finish an article feeling satisfied
grammarElements.label: 주절 (분사구문)

grammarElements.text: only to discover, when asked to explain it, that her understanding was illusory
grammarElements.label: only to 결과 부정사 (삽입 시간절 + that 명사절)

grammarElements.text: a phenomenon so common that researchers have named it the "illusion of explanatory depth"
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 동격구 (so ... that 결과절)

vocabulary.example: Without metacognition, understanding may be illusory.
The illusion of explanatory depth means we think we understand more than we do.

expressions.usage: "only to + 동사"로 예상치 못한 결과를 나타내는 결과 부정사

expressions.phrase: so common that researchers have named it
expressions.usage: "so ... that" 결과절로 현상의 보편성을 강조

patterns.pattern: Without [awareness], [a person] may [feel satisfied], only to discover [that understanding was illusory] — a phenomenon so common that researchers have named it "[term]"
patterns.explanation: "Without"으로 결핍 조건을 설정하고, "only to discover"로 반전을 제시한 뒤, 대시와 "so common that"으로 연구 용어를 도입하는 패턴.
examples: Without critical awareness, a voter may leave a rally feeling inspired, only to discover, when pressed for details, that her conviction was superficial — a phenomenon so common that psychologists have named it the "halo effect."

Without financial literacy, an investor may feel confident after reading headlines, only to discover, when markets shift, that his strategy was baseless — a phenomenon so common that economists have named it "overconfidence bias."

drillQuestions.question: "Illusory" means based on _____.
"Only to discover" introduces a _____ outcome.
The "illusion of explanatory depth" means we overestimate our _____.
"So common that researchers have named it" emphasizes its _____.

sentences.notes: "only to discover"의 결과 부정사가 만족에서 환멸로의 반전을 극적으로 제시한다. "illusion of explanatory depth"가 에세이의 논지를 뒷받침하는 경험적 증거로 기능하며, 독자 자신의 경험과 연결될 수 있는 보편적 현상을 지적한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Without metacognitive awareness, a reader may finish an article feeling satisfied
koreanAlignment.ko: 메타인지적 인식 없이는 독자가 만족감을 느끼며 기사를 마칠 수 있지만

koreanAlignment.en: only to discover, when asked to explain it
koreanAlignment.ko: 그것을 설명하라는 요청을 받았을 때 발견하게 된다

koreanAlignment.en: that her understanding was illusory
koreanAlignment.ko: 자신의 이해가 환상적이었음을

koreanAlignment.en: a phenomenon so common that researchers have named it the "illusion of explanatory depth"
koreanAlignment.ko: 연구자들이 "설명적 깊이의 착각"이라고 이름 붙인 만큼 흔한 현상

pronunciationNotes: illusory: /ɪˈluːsəri/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "일루서리"에 가깝다.
metacognitive: /ˌmetəˈkɑːɡnɪtɪv/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "메타카그니티브"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The remedy is not more information but better habits of mind: the discipline to ask "Do I actually understand this, or do I merely recognize it?", the humility to admit confusion, and the patience to pursue understanding even when information alone would suffice.
sentences.translation: 치료법은 더 많은 정보가 아니라 더 나은 마음의 습관이다: "내가 이것을 실제로 이해하는가, 아니면 단지 인식하는 것인가?"라고 묻는 훈련, 혼란을 인정하는 겸손, 그리고 정보만으로 충분할 때에도 이해를 추구하는 인내.

grammarElements.text: The remedy
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is not more information but better habits of mind
grammarElements.label: 보어 (not A but B)

grammarElements.text: the discipline to ask "Do I actually understand this, or do I merely recognize it?"
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 동격 1 (직접 인용)

grammarElements.text: the humility to admit confusion
grammarElements.label: 동격 2

grammarElements.text: and the patience to pursue understanding even when information alone would suffice
grammarElements.label: 동격 3 (even when 양보절)

vocabulary.example: The remedy is better habits, not more data.
Sometimes information alone would suffice, but understanding is worth pursuing.

expressions.usage: 인지적 태도와 실천을 총칭하는 교육학적 표현

expressions.phrase: even when information alone would suffice
expressions.usage: "even when" 양보절로 이해 추구의 무조건성을 강조

patterns.pattern: The remedy is not more [X] but better [habits]: the discipline to [ask Y], the humility to [admit Z], and the patience to [pursue W even when X would suffice]
patterns.explanation: "not A but B"로 해결책을 재정의하고, 콜론 뒤 삼중 동격(discipline, humility, patience)으로 구체적 덕목을 나열하는 처방적 패턴.
examples: The remedy is not more meetings but better communication: the discipline to listen before responding, the humility to acknowledge when one is wrong, and the patience to build consensus even when a quick decision would suffice.

The remedy is not more data but better judgment: the discipline to ask whether the numbers tell the full story, the humility to admit what the data cannot capture, and the patience to investigate further even when preliminary results would suffice.

drillQuestions.question: "Habits of mind" refers to cognitive _____.
The three habits are discipline, humility, and _____.
"Suffice" means to be _____.
"Do I understand or merely recognize" distinguishes _____ from comprehension.

sentences.notes: "not more information but better habits of mind"가 에세이의 처방적 전환을 수행한다. "discipline, humility, patience"의 삼중 덕목이 이해 추구의 윤리적 차원을 추가하며, 직접 인용된 자기 질문이 독자에게 즉시 적용 가능한 도구를 제공한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The remedy is not more information but better habits of mind
koreanAlignment.ko: 치료법은 더 많은 정보가 아니라 더 나은 마음의 습관이다

koreanAlignment.en: the discipline to ask "Do I actually understand this, or do I merely recognize it?"
koreanAlignment.ko: "내가 이것을 실제로 이해하는가, 아니면 단지 인식하는 것인가?"라고 묻는 훈련

koreanAlignment.en: the humility to admit confusion
koreanAlignment.ko: 혼란을 인정하는 겸손

koreanAlignment.en: and the patience to pursue understanding even when information alone would suffice
koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 정보만으로 충분할 때에도 이해를 추구하는 인내

pronunciationNotes: remedy: /ˈremədi/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "레머디"에 가깝다.
suffice: /səˈfaɪs/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "서파이스"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: In language learning, this distinction acquires special urgency, for the learner who memorizes vocabulary and grammar rules has acquired information about the language, while the learner who can think and feel in the target language has achieved understanding of it — and the distance between those two accomplishments is vast.
sentences.translation: 언어 학습에서 이 구분은 특별한 긴급성을 획득하는데, 어휘와 문법 규칙을 암기하는 학습자는 언어에 대한 정보를 획득한 것인 반면, 목표 언어로 생각하고 느낄 수 있는 학습자는 그것에 대한 이해를 달성한 것이며 — 그 두 성취 사이의 거리는 광대하다.

grammarElements.text: In language learning, this distinction acquires special urgency
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: for the learner who memorizes vocabulary and grammar rules has acquired information about the language
grammarElements.label: for 이유절 1

grammarElements.text: while the learner who can think and feel in the target language has achieved understanding of it
grammarElements.label: while 대조절

grammarElements.text: and the distance between those two accomplishments is vast
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 결론절

vocabulary.example: In language learning, the distinction acquires special urgency.
The distance between knowing about and understanding is vast.

expressions.usage: 특정 맥락에서 논점이 더 중요해짐을 나타내는 학술적 표현

expressions.phrase: information about the language vs. understanding of it
expressions.meaning: 언어에 대한 정보 vs. 그것에 대한 이해
expressions.usage: 에세이의 핵심 이분법을 언어 학습에 직접 적용하는 대비

patterns.pattern: In [specific domain], this distinction acquires special urgency, for [person who does A] has acquired information about [X], while [person who does B] has achieved understanding of it — and the distance between those two is vast
patterns.explanation: 에세이의 일반 논지를 특정 영역(언어 학습)에 적용하고, "information about X vs. understanding of it" 대비로 구체화한 뒤, "vast"로 간극을 강조하는 적용 패턴.
examples: In medicine, this distinction acquires special urgency, for the student who memorizes symptoms and treatments has acquired information about diseases, while the clinician who can read a patient's body with diagnostic intuition has achieved understanding of them — and the distance between those two competencies is vast.

In music, this distinction acquires special urgency, for the student who memorizes scales and theory has acquired information about harmony, while the musician who can improvise with emotional depth has achieved understanding of it — and the distance between those two abilities is vast.

drillQuestions.question: "Special urgency" means the distinction is _____ important here.
Memorizing grammar gives information _____ the language.
Thinking in the target language represents _____.
"Vast" emphasizes that the distance is _____.

sentences.notes: 에세이의 일반 논지를 독자의 직접적 경험(언어 학습)에 적용하여 반성적 효과를 만든다. "information about vs. understanding of"가 에세이의 핵심 이분법을 정확히 반복하고, "vast"가 두 성취 사이의 질적 간극을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: In language learning, this distinction acquires special urgency
koreanAlignment.ko: 언어 학습에서 이 구분은 특별한 긴급성을 획득하는데

koreanAlignment.en: for the learner who memorizes vocabulary and grammar rules has acquired information about the language
koreanAlignment.ko: 어휘와 문법 규칙을 암기하는 학습자는 언어에 대한 정보를 획득한 것인 반면

koreanAlignment.en: while the learner who can think and feel in the target language has achieved understanding of it
koreanAlignment.ko: 목표 언어로 생각하고 느낄 수 있는 학습자는 그것에 대한 이해를 달성한 것이며

koreanAlignment.en: and the distance between those two accomplishments is vast
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 두 성취 사이의 거리는 광대하다

pronunciationNotes: urgency: /ˈɜːrdʒənsi/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "어전시"에 가깝다.
accomplishments: /əˈkɑːmplɪʃmənts/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어캄플리쉬먼츠"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The aspiration, then, should not be to know more but to understand better, recognizing that understanding is not a destination but a practice — an ongoing, effortful, and ultimately rewarding conversation between the mind and the material it seeks to comprehend.
sentences.translation: 따라서 열망은 더 많이 아는 것이 아니라 더 잘 이해하는 것이어야 하며, 이해는 목적지가 아니라 실천 — 마음과 그것이 이해하고자 하는 자료 사이의 지속적이고, 노력을 요하며, 궁극적으로 보람 있는 대화 — 임을 인식해야 한다.

grammarElements.text: The aspiration, then,
grammarElements.label: 주어 (삽입 부사)

grammarElements.text: should not be to know more but to understand better
grammarElements.label: 보어 (not A but B)

grammarElements.text: recognizing that understanding is not a destination but a practice
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (not A but B)

grammarElements.text: an ongoing, effortful, and ultimately rewarding conversation between the mind and the material it seeks to comprehend
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 동격구

vocabulary.example: The aspiration should be understanding, not mere knowledge accumulation.
Understanding is an effortful but rewarding practice.

expressions.phrase: not a destination but a practice
expressions.usage: 이해를 완결 상태가 아닌 지속적 활동으로 재정의하는 비유적 표현

expressions.phrase: a conversation between the mind and the material
expressions.usage: 이해를 대화적 관계로 비유하는 문학적 마무리 표현

patterns.pattern: The aspiration should not be to [know more] but to [understand better], recognizing that [understanding] is not [a destination] but [a practice] — [an ongoing, effortful, and ultimately rewarding conversation between X and Y]
patterns.explanation: "not A but B"를 이중으로 사용하여 (1) 열망을 재정의하고 (2) 이해를 재정의한 뒤, 대시 뒤의 확장된 비유로 에세이를 마무리하는 최종 패턴.
examples: The aspiration should not be to accumulate credentials but to develop competence, recognizing that expertise is not a title but a practice — an ongoing, challenging, and ultimately fulfilling engagement between the practitioner and the problems she seeks to solve.

The aspiration should not be to reach certainty but to deepen inquiry, recognizing that wisdom is not an endpoint but a practice — an ongoing, humble, and ultimately enriching dialogue between what we know and what we have yet to discover.

drillQuestions.question: "Not a destination but a practice" means understanding is _____.
"Effortful" means requiring _____.
Understanding is compared to a _____ between mind and material.
This sentence serves as the essay's _____.

sentences.notes: 이중 "not A but B" 구조가 에세이의 핵심 재정의를 수행한다: (1) 열망을 "know more"에서 "understand better"로, (2) 이해를 "destination"에서 "practice"로. "conversation between the mind and the material"이라는 대화적 비유가 에세이를 따뜻하고 인간적인 어조로 마무리한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The aspiration, then, should not be to know more but to understand better
koreanAlignment.ko: 따라서 열망은 더 많이 아는 것이 아니라 더 잘 이해하는 것이어야 하며

koreanAlignment.en: recognizing that understanding is not a destination but a practice
koreanAlignment.ko: 이해는 목적지가 아니라 실천임을 인식해야 한다

koreanAlignment.en: an ongoing, effortful, and ultimately rewarding conversation
koreanAlignment.ko: 지속적이고, 노력을 요하며, 궁극적으로 보람 있는 대화

koreanAlignment.en: between the mind and the material it seeks to comprehend
koreanAlignment.ko: 마음과 그것이 이해하고자 하는 자료 사이의

pronunciationNotes: aspiration: /ˌæspəˈreɪʃən/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "애스퍼레이션"에 가깝다.
effortful: /ˈefərtfʊl/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "에퍼트풀"에 가깝다.
